reasoning: The base value (as of 2025-10-26) corresponds to the Oct 22 weekly value of 905,074 million, which represents a highly elevated local peak following mid-October tax receipts. Treasury balances typically drift downward in the last week of October as everyday outlays drain cash prior to major end-of-month debt issuance, making a decrease by Nov 2 (reflecting Oct 29 data) likely. For the Nov 25 resolution date (reflecting mid-November data), the massive structural payments historically occurring on Nov 1 push balances lower. However, given the longer one-month horizon, uncertainty increases, and the probability moves somewhat closer to 0.5. For the January 24, 2026 date, Treasury announced an end-of-December 2025 target cash balance of $850 billion. Typical mid-January estimated tax receipts generally add between $70B and $100B, raising the prospect that the late-January balance could reach $920B to $950B. This brings the expected value remarkably close to the 905k threshold, pushing the probability up to 0.45 to reflect a near coin-toss.
